meaning_english: To support or confirm a theory, story, or evidence. Synonyms: Corroborate, Substantiate, Validate.
To cause a particular reaction or to elicit a response. Synonyms: Evoke, Elicit, Provoke.
To look around for something, such as an idea, excuse, or solution. Synonyms: Search, Scour, Seek.
To make a feeling or a situation less intense or strong. Synonyms: Subdue, Quell, Restrain.
To use a resource, such as information, experience, or money. Synonyms: Utilize, Employ, Exploit.
To strongly encourage someone to do something unwise or dangerous. Synonyms: Incite, Goad, Urge.
To defend yourself against an attack, criticism, or difficult questions. Synonyms: Ward off, Repel, Deflect.
To avoid dealing with a mistake or unpleasant fact by ignoring it. Synonyms: Mask, Conceal, Veil.
To reach an agreement after long or difficult discussions. Synonyms: Negotiate, Finalize, Settle.
To mention or remember something from the past. Synonyms: Revert, Recall, Reminisce.
To become covered with ice or to become cold and unfriendly. Synonyms: Freeze, Solidify.
To drop or abandon something that is no longer useful. Synonyms: Discard, Dump, Shed.
To maintain a safe distance or to withhold information. Synonyms: Withhold, Restrain, Retain.
To establish a rule, law, or principle officially. Synonyms: Prescribe, Enact, Mandate.
To be good enough or to reach the required standard. Synonyms: Match, Qualify, Equal.
To fall asleep unintentionally, especially while sitting. Synonyms: Doze off, Nap.
To admit that you have done something wrong or embarrassing. Synonyms: Confess, Admit.
To stop using or producing something gradually. Synonyms: Discontinue, Withdraw.
To survive a dangerous illness or a very difficult situation. Synonyms: Recover, Survive.
To become less noisy or to make someone silent. Synonyms: Hush, Mute, Quell.
To mention something unpleasant from the past that was forgotten. Synonyms: Revive, Recall.
To start doing something with determination. Synonyms: Commence, Undertake, Begin.
To support or strengthen something that is weak or failing. Synonyms: Bolster, Reinforce, Support.
To become gradually smaller or weaker until it stops. Synonyms: Diminish, Wane, Decline.
To cause something new to start or to herald a new era. Synonyms: Introduce, Herald.
To improve the appearance of something to make it more attractive. Synonyms: Revamp, Refurbish.
To wait until an unpleasant event or situation stops. Synonyms: Endure, Wait.
To cross something out with an 'X' or to delete it. Synonyms: Delete, Strike out.
To give up possession of something or to reveal information. Synonyms: Surrender, Relinquish.
To focus all your attention on one particular thing. Synonyms: Pinpoint, Target, Focus.
